activities.name: Happy New Year with Herve
activities.description: And an excellent mid-run coffee at Zoka

activities.name: New Years run with Chris
activities.description: She calls these her “Run Interrupted” runs. If you follow her on Instagram you will see the results

activities.name: Half of Bridle Trails with Jonathan
Herve joined me for a classic Phinney run
activities.description: Good turnaround after yesterday afternoon’s 12.

activities.name: Single track happiness on Grand Ridge with Mintu
Gentle rain in the fading light
Flat 4.
activities.description: Unsustainably fast, so let’s call it “mile intervals”

activities.name: Outer GL+WP loop.
activities.description: Checking mileage and planning something weird for the future.

activities.name: Revisiting favorite spots in Stockholm
activities.description: Only 7 hours of sunshine in January, but I got a little of it!

activities.name: Figure 8 to Redmond town
activities.description: Fresh legs due to lack of running in Sweden. Felt pretty good.

activities.name: Chris’ longest run.
activities.description: No stops! She will be ready for the Tunnel/Viaduct 8K in two weeks!

activities.name: Bonkity bonk all the way home
PR in the fog
activities.description: 30s faster than I have ever done this route. Legs felt noticeably looser than normal. Been taking CoQ10 for 4 days. Could that be it?

activities.name: Seven brides, seven brothers, and Mintu.
activities.description: Seven miles up, then seven back doing every mile 10 secs faster than the previous. Fun little project

activities.name: Chris sets a new distance record. She is ready for next weekend’s Tunnel/Viaduct 8K
boustrophēdon
activities.description: Look it up. And yes, it was very fast for me.

activities.name: Longer makes stronger they say
activities.description: Well actually, I say it. I annoy my friends by saying it.

activities.name: Steamboat Springs snowshoe in deep powder
Tunnel/viaduct run with Chris and 29,000 friends
activities.description: Battery St tunnel and brunch were the highlights!

activities.name: Seeking salt on slippery sidewalks
activities.description: Ice makes you go slow and perhaps forces you into better form. That’s my theory anyway.

activities.name: Needed that!
activities.description: 4 days of no running due to work crunch and snow made me stir crazy

activities.name: Snow hike with Chris and Grace
Long flat run with Mintu
activities.description: There is such a thing as too flat for comfort, when you use exactly the same muscles every stride for 150 minutes.

activities.name: Longer isn’t yet making stronger
activities.description: As usual, after about 16 miles I started to lose it. Perhaps my problem is that I am unwilling to embrace the suffering.

activities.name: Recovery runs are the best
activities.description: No expectations.

activities.name: Cold, windy and sunny. Perfect conditions
activities.description: Great run, but went out a little too fast (cough PR) so had to stop a couple of times on the way back.

activities.name: Jonathan’s pace + some back and forth
Cantering through a figure 8 in Bridle Trails
activities.description: Fastest pace ever through here.

activities.name: Don’t beat yourself up
activities.description: Only 1/3 of the distance I thought I would run, but so what?

activities.name: Too nice this afternoon not to run
Down down to Redmond Town
activities.description: Too fast going down to come back up for a PR, but close

activities.name: Snow stroll with David, Laura, Tim, Barbara and Chris
Galena x-country classic ski with Chris
Bluebird day on the Harriman trail with Chris
Steep climb up unnamed hill
activities.description: Very firm snow. Great traction in snowshoes, but lots of exposure.

activities.name: Smoothly does it in the drizzle
Smooth Six
activities.description: Should have recorded as an 8:25 pace. I accidentally pressed the wrong button on my watch

activities.name: Trail intervals with Mintu and Anita
Run interrupted
activities.description: By a hot spot in my new shoes

activities.name: Once more upon the gentle hills of Tangletown
activities.description: With a stop, pretending my shoelace needed tieing

activities.name: Jog/walk with Jonathan in the brutal 70F sunshine
activities.description: As my friends know, that’s about 20 degrees hotter than I like. But hey - summer is coming!

activities.name: Ballard and Burke with Mintu
activities.description: And some Green Lake before and after to cheer on the Dizzydaze runners. Inspiring!

activities.name: Chris runs Interlaken, and a bit more
That old rough diamond with a side of drizzle
Short and smooth
activities.description: But not as easy as it should have been.

activities.name: When there’s no time there’s time for intervals
Trail Half Marathon event with Mintu, Anita, Priya and Marielle
Chris runs Kirkland trail, and then we are waylaid by Burgermaster
Seeking shade in the brutal 60 degree sunshine.
activities.description: A few “sun breaks” to cool down.

activities.name: Quarter pizza with a timing error
activities.description: 8:20-30 pace... smooth

activities.name: Will run for teff
activities.description: Cafe Laurelhurst, the long way.

activities.name: Tough after teff
activities.description: Though perhaps the sluggishness in my legs was caused by the effort required to get to the teff yesterday.

activities.name: A bit here, a bit there. With Mintu and Jason!
This one’s for Didier
activities.description: I was forest bathing while he was running the last 3 miles of a very tough Boston Marathon. I salute you Didier!

activities.name: Trying to run like Gene Dykes
activities.description: Last week, when Gene was tapering for Boston he ran really slowly, but with a 177 cadence. I usually do 165, and tried as hard as I could to achieve 175 today, but not quite. Will try again.

activities.name: Strides with Jonathan
activities.description: Left my watch at home

activities.name: A double dip of forest bathing
activities.description: Good recovery from Saturday’s big exertion.

activities.name: A little sluggish and a little warm
Archana’s first run back
activities.description: Great first effort!

activities.name: And a bit more by myself
activities.description: High cadence, but stoppish.

activities.name: Green green Cougar Mountain with Mintu
Getting over a cold
activities.description: And sore hamstrings too for some reason.

activities.name: Sunny commute on the Sammamish River trail
activities.description: Still not 100% but any run is a good run.

activities.name: Vancouver 8K with Chris
activities.description: This is the one she has been training for! A beautiful morning in a beautiful city. Thank you Mintu and Anita for bringing us up here!

activities.name: Seeking shade in the forest
activities.description: But still, a handful of cool down stops

activities.name: Through the Microsoft forest with Jonathan
The gun that shot Hamilton?
activities.description: Last run before we leave for Scotland!

activities.name: A wee walk by a bonnie loch
Little jog among the giants of Glencoe
Hike up the glen and into the Lost Valley
Finally... some Scottish weather
activities.description: Very similar to Pacific Northwest weather.

activities.name: Boden Boo and the mighty Clyde
activities.description: Wish we had been able to spend more than an afternoon in Glasgow. Scotland: we will return!

activities.name: Wilberton Hill evening 8K trail to grill
activities.description: Not such a great effort, but glad to have done it.

activities.name: Bridle Trails x 4 on a beautiful morning
activities.description: Tried to do each lap faster than the last and almost succeeded. Good effort though as I’ve never done more than 2 laps of this place before.

activities.name: Exploring the Chuckanut trail
activities.description: I was in Bellingham for work, so...

activities.name: “I’ll point. You shoot” said Chris
activities.description: Running in the arboretum with the painter of trees goes like that.

activities.name: Mintu and the horror of the sunshine!
Going easy on the pizza
activities.description: Felt better than the last few runs. Hamstring slightly better, maybe because I was going slower.

activities.name: A little road, a little trail, a little hill.
activities.description: Last run before Sunday’s tunnel marathon

activities.name: Light at the end of the tunnel marathon with Mintu, Didier
activities.description:
And amazing support from Herve and Chris
Resolution: I’m a 13-20 mile guy. Half marathons and trail 25Ks are what I will stick with for a while. Happiness comes with self awareness.

activities.name: Early morning at Lake Serene
activities.description: Tip: if you go on a nice weekend day, plan to leave the trailhead no later than I did (8:00am). When I was coming down I crossed paths with 100-150 people most  heading for that little slab of granite overlooking the lake.

activities.name: Baby steps
activities.description: I will spare you the details about why I haven’t run in more than two weeks, but “Shorter and slower” will be my mantra for the foreseeable future.

activities.name: Better baby steps.
activities.description: A little longer, a little faster.

activities.name: Jonathan’s pace is now my pace
Sometimes you just need to stop.
Western States 5
activities.description: We all do what we can.

activities.name: The joy of bonking
activities.description: So to speak.

activities.name: Rambling around Timberline
activities.description: And jogging the first half mile of the Hood-to-Coast. Yes friends — we will enter the lottery for the 2020 HTC.

activities.name: Zigzag canyon and Paradise Park from Timberline Lodge
activities.description: With Chris, Steve, Sonja, Tamara and Nick

activities.name: Downhill with Tamara
activities.description: 2200’ of drop between Timberline Lodge and Government camp. That’s a lot!

activities.name: Ramble around Mt Tabor with Liz, Rose, and Chris
Humid quarter pizza
activities.description: Lots of stops but decent speed when I was moving. Best run in a while.

activities.name: Sometimes you just have to stop
activities.description: Don’t have a sweet snack 90 mins before a run.

activities.name: And sometimes you can run a little bit more
activities.description: After walking for a mile.

activities.name: Jonathon and the very sticky wicket
activities.description: Super humid.

activities.name: Hike/swim in the Little Wenatchee canyon with Rick, Lyn, Chris and Baloo!
Picnic lunch with Rick, Lyn, Chris and Baloo
activities.description: Above the White River which feeds into Lake Wenatchee

activities.name: Full Bridle Trails Loop with Anita
activities.description: Even if the track seems to stop half way. Not sure why

activities.name: Too hot to trot...
activities.description: ... the full Bridle Trails loop.

activities.name: Seeking shade, but still stopping a lot to cool down.
Forest Bathing in Seattle with Jesper, Mintu, and Anita
Contours of Tangletown
activities.description: Very sluggish.

activities.name: Some like it cloudy.
activities.description: Me, for example. And being below 70 degrees helped a lot too.

activities.name: Chris sets a PR for Green Lake!
Lumpy Ridge Loop with Dave and Ron
Chiquita Peak (13,000 feet) with Dave and Ron
activities.description: Not so tough when the trailhead is at 11,000 ft!

activities.name: Lakes and a waterfall in Rocky Mountain National Park, with Ron and Dave
Hike Estes Cone, then run (-ish) down.
activities.description: Day 4 of adventures with Ron and Dave.

activities.name: Leg stretcher with Chris.
activities.description: Next stop Sydney!

activities.name: Bush tracks of Greenwich, Woollstoncraft and Balls Point
activities.description: Windy and cool. Gotta love those Sydney Harbor winter bush runs. If I was living in Sydney I would definitively join the Northside Runners Group, who I met out on their regular Saturday 16K bush run. Great group!

activities.name: Mark and I take on the short steep Cascades Track
activities.description: And one of us takes a fall. It wasn’t Mark.

activities.name: Cooler MS Trail, but still not cool enough
activities.description: Stopped a number of times to cool down.

activities.name: West Side (of Tiger) Story
activities.description: With Mintu, Anita, and Mark! 2nd fall in a week. 2nd fall in 8 years of trail running I blame my hamstring.

activities.name: Introducing the Hamstring Health Index: HH=2
activities.description: 0=too painful to run or even walk fast; 1=I ran but maybe shouldn’t have. 2=continuous mild pain that didn’t get any worse during run. 3=only hurts on the uphills. 4=no pain at all if I keep the steps short. 5=full functionality including strides.  Today was a 2.

activities.name: Hitch-then-hike with Peter G who is in the middle of a full Wonderland
Hike, bushwhack, scramble, run, hitch, run on Silver Peak with Mark D
activities.description: Slow going off trail, but a four mile ride in the back of a truck certainly improved our pace! Thanks Mark for a memorable adventure. Recently cropped the back half of it to not count the hitchhike

activities.name: Standard Bridle Trails with Mintu and Anita
Jog to drop off Jonathan at Pro Club
Sometimes you need to start to realize that you didn’t need to start.
Ran into Steve and Tamara... so changed plans. HH=2
Finding a little wild in the city
Hello cool rain. I missed you!
activities.description: With Jonathan

activities.name: Chris hits the pace target!
activities.description: 11min pace was the goal.

activities.name: One hour was enough for me. Mighty Mintu motored on.
Run, bonk, walk, jog
activities.description: Oh well.

activities.name: Quarter pizza at my new slower pace
Ain’t got it.
activities.description: Still too hot after 2 miles

activities.name: 2 Daves run the campus trail
activities.description: Keeping up with Dave Armour, or at least trying to.

activities.name: The Watershed with Mintu and Anita
activities.description: Trying to be stoic about slowing down so much.

activities.name: Finding the forty fives
activities.description: Under gentle rain

activities.name: Better. Felt rough but finally made it under 9 min/mile again
Mr Efficient: Running to and from a PT appointment.
52 degrees F. I love you!
activities.description: Coolest weather in 6 months = best run in 6 months.

activities.name: Disco and Magno with Minto and Anito
Soon the night
activities.description: Making the most of the fall afternoons.

activities.name: MS 5K forest trail in the cool clear afternoon
Bonkity bonk all the way home
Solid Bridle Trails with Peter Gaylord. He makes it look effortless!
Mintu runs 9; David runs 7. We do what we can.
Off leash!
activities.description: Left the watch at home so as not to be obsessed about my slow pace. Didn’t really help, but it was nice to be out.

activities.name: The smile on the face of Microsoft.
activities.description: Easy pace; route chosen so that I didn’t Have to stop at a single light or crossing.

activities.name: Will run long for sweet potato frites
Was going to make it pretty...
activities.description: But ran into an old mate I hadn’t seen for many years. Had a nice visit with him and his family mid-run

activities.name: 7th run in 7 days. Getting my base back!
My heart is in good shape apparently
activities.description: Getting credit for my eco-stress test with before and after nuclear imaging. Very fancy!

activities.name: Sometimes you quit early
activities.description: And don’t feel bad about it.

activities.name: The most beautiful road run in the area?
activities.description: Mintu, me, and a million autumn leaves.

activities.name: Hike to Dirty Harry’s Balcony with Chris and Jay
Steady wind and rain
activities.description: Brought to you by October in Seattle.

activities.name: Broken Diamond and a hitch up the hill.
activities.description: Thank you Cathy from Operations!

activities.name: Walk with Chris around Blackwater pond.
Ride through the dunes and beech forests of the Provincelands with Chris and Grace
A little road, a little trail; a little rain, a little gale.
Too fast to sustain. Call it irregular intervals.
Dusk into dark in the forest
activities.description: Slow down and pick up your feet! Luckily I didn’t fall.

activities.name: Rattlesnake ledge, the long way.
activities.description: With Mintu, strong as ever.

activities.name: Jonathon’s pace
activities.description: And very strange gps errors too.

activities.name: Second run of the day. Smooth.
activities.description: Felt good going slow and steady.

activities.name: Short should be easy... but sometimes it isn’t
Flat 6, then hilly 4 through Mathews Beach with Mintu.
Hooray for the “Studio Runners”
activities.description: Fun with Katie, Muneeb and others.

activities.name: Long locks loop lumbering alongside lively Mintu
Running with Chris and testing Minecraft Earth
activities.description: Which I work on, indirectly.

activities.name: Checking out night mode on my new phone
Thursday Studio Runners
activities.description: Glorious sunny day on the trail.

activities.name: Snoqualmie Valley Trail to Tokul trestle with Mintu
activities.description: Felt good... a little longer and better than anything in the last six months.

activities.name: Late fall at Green Lake with Chris
Exploring Tillamook Head
activities.description: Verdict: rocky, rooty, and a bit muddy. No views. Turned my ankle. Lesson learned. Happy Thanksgiving!

activities.name: Rested legs went faster than aging lungs.
activities.description: Not bad otherwise.

activities.name: Aerobically with Jonathan and Studio Runners.
Dusk to dark on the fork
activities.description: Felt tougher than it should have.

activities.name: Herve runs again!
activities.description: He’s acting surprisingly sensibly and only joined Mintu and I for the last two miles.

activities.name: Testing some new trail shoes in the Microsoft forest
activities.description: Altra Lone Peak 4. I understand you need to ease in to these zero-drop shoes. So far so good.

activities.name: Quarter pizza with a soggy crust
Easy 5 on trail and roads with Studio Runners
A little lunchtime lollipop
activities.description: For my birthday.

activities.name: Soggy streets and sunny friends
activities.description: 6 miles with Mintu, then Herve and Anita joined us for 3 in the Arboretum.

activities.name: More trail shoe testing: short and sloppy in Woodland Park
activities.description: Verdict: Lone Peak Altras are keepers

activities.name: Rough diamond
activities.description: Feeling a little looser in the legs than I have for most of this year. Did one downhill mile at a 7:30 place which I used to do, but haven’t approached recently. Nice.

activities.name: Encountering Otters at Meadowbrook Pond
activities.description: With my mate Mintu.

activities.name: With Chris... dramatic light on Green Lake
Family and friends snowshoe at Stevens Pass
activities.description: These are the days we remember.

activities.name: To Discovery Park with Mintu and Steve T!
activities.description: Great to have Steve back on the team!

activities.name: Hooray! Yearly distance goal achieved.
activities.description: Beautiful morning at Discovery Park with my best running buddies Mintu and Anita

activities.name: Track workout with coach Gigi
activities.description: Modified ladder: 100m, 2, 4, 5, 4, 2, 1 and then a slower 800m.

activities.name: Slow solo slog
activities.description: At least it felt like a bit of a slog.
